mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of rising-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market slowdown that reduces consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in >70% of triggered instances, or the unemployment threshold fails to predict return direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over individual series, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 when both series move in the same direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output utilization, which signals strong near-term earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive accuracy during expansion phases, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of rising INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a reliable leading indicator of aggregate income growth and consumer demand, which consistently supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows, or payroll growth fails to correlate with 20-day return direction at significance levels.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond the 2.5% annual threshold while policy rates remain suppressed, markets price in aggressive future tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day forward returns in >65% of threshold-crossing events, or the inflation-rate combination fails to predict return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in industrial production and real GDP confirms robust supply-demand balance, supporting stable margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in predicting returns, or the joint signal underperforms single-series baselines in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth signals immediate consumer spending strength that directly translates into higher quarterly earnings for consumer-facing sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of accelerating retail sales periods.
